entry.title: Re: Sharing our custom copy programs based on read and create BAPI
entry.content:
Here's a program to copy a purchase document (header and items only, but it's easy to extend) via BAPI_PO_GETDETAIL2 and BAPI_PO_CREATE1.

I'm using the generic method SET_DEFAULT_BAPIUPDATE_FLAGS to automatically define the value of the parameter POITEMX.

There's also a CHANGE method to copy the first item in an existing purchase order which I used to make sure that it's not needed to pass the other items).

I wondered what the field PO_ITEMX of parameter POITEMX was for, it has apparently no effect, but I asked the question here in case you wonder too: BAPI_PO_CHANGE meaning of X key fields of the X ta... - SAP Community.

REPORT z_po_copy.

PARAMETERS ebeln TYPE ekpo-ebeln DEFAULT '9500000137'.

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION DEFERRED.

DATA go_app TYPE REF TO lcl_app.

LOAD-OF-PROGRAM.
  CALL METHOD lcl_app=>('CREATE')
    RECEIVING
      result = go_app.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  CALL METHOD go_app->('START_OF_SELECTION').
*  CALL METHOD go_app->('CHANGE').

CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS change.

    CLASS-METHODS create
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO lcl_app.

    METHODS start_of_selection.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS set_default_bapiupdate_flags
      IMPORTING is_bapi_parameter   TYPE any
      CHANGING  cs_bapi_parameter_x TYPE any.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD create.
    result = NEW lcl_app( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD start_of_selection.
    DATA(purchaseorder) = EXACT ebeln( ebeln ).
    DATA(items) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(account_assignment) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(schedules) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(history) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(item_texts) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(header_texts) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(services) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(confirmations) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(service_texts) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(extensions) = VALUE selkz( ).
    DATA(po_header) = VALUE bapiekkol( ).
    DATA(po_address) = VALUE bapiaddress( ).
    TYPES po_header_texts            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekkotx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_items                   TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekpo WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_account_assignment TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekkn WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_schedules          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapieket WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_confirmations      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekes WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_texts              TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekpotx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_history            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekbe WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_history_totals     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekbes WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_limits             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiesuh WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_contract_limits    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiesuc WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_services           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiesll WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_item_srv_accass_values  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapieskl WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES return                     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapireturn WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES po_services_texts          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapieslltx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES extensionout               TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiparex WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    DATA po_header_texts            TYPE po_header_texts.
    DATA po_items                   TYPE po_items.
    DATA po_item_account_assignment TYPE po_item_account_assignment.
    DATA po_item_schedules          TYPE po_item_schedules.
    DATA po_item_confirmations      TYPE po_item_confirmations.
    DATA po_item_texts              TYPE po_item_texts.
    DATA po_item_history            TYPE po_item_history.
    DATA po_item_history_totals     TYPE po_item_history_totals.
    DATA po_item_limits             TYPE po_item_limits.
    DATA po_item_contract_limits    TYPE po_item_contract_limits.
    DATA po_item_services           TYPE po_item_services.
    DATA po_item_srv_accass_values  TYPE po_item_srv_accass_values.
    DATA return                     TYPE return.
    DATA po_services_texts          TYPE po_services_texts.
    DATA extensionout               TYPE extensionout.

    items = 'X'.
    account_assignment = 'X'.
    schedules          = 'X'.
    history            = 'X'.
    item_texts         = 'X'.
    header_texts       = 'X'.
    services           = 'X'.
    confirmations      = 'X'.
    service_texts      = 'X'.
    extensions         = 'X'.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_GETDETAIL2'
      EXPORTING
        purchaseorder              = purchaseorder
        items                      = items
        account_assignment         = account_assignment
        schedules                  = schedules
        history                    = history
        item_texts                 = item_texts
        header_texts               = header_texts
        services                   = services
        confirmations              = confirmations
        service_texts              = service_texts
        extensions                 = extensions
      IMPORTING
        po_header                  = po_header
        po_address                 = po_address
      TABLES
        po_header_texts            = po_header_texts
        po_items                   = po_items
        po_item_account_assignment = po_item_account_assignment
        po_item_schedules          = po_item_schedules
        po_item_confirmations      = po_item_confirmations
        po_item_texts              = po_item_texts
        po_item_history            = po_item_history
        po_item_history_totals     = po_item_history_totals
        po_item_limits             = po_item_limits
        po_item_contract_limits    = po_item_contract_limits
        po_item_services           = po_item_services
        po_item_srv_accass_values  = po_item_srv_accass_values
        return                     = return
        po_services_texts          = po_services_texts
        extensionout               = extensionout.

    DATA(poheader) = VALUE bapimepoheader( ).
    DATA(poheaderx)    = VALUE bapimepoheaderx( ).
    DATA(poaddrvendor) = VALUE bapimepoaddrvendor( ).
    DATA(testrun) = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(memory_uncomplete) = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(memory_complete) = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(poexpimpheader)  = VALUE bapieikp( ).
    DATA(poexpimpheaderx) = VALUE bapieikpx( ).
    DATA(versions) = VALUE bapimedcm( ).
    DATA(no_messaging) = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(no_message_req) = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(no_authority) = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(no_price_from_po) = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(park_complete)    = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(park_uncomplete)  = VALUE char1( ).
    DATA(exppurchaseorder) = VALUE ebeln( ).
    DATA(expheader) = VALUE bapimepoheader( ).
    DATA(exppoexpimpheader) = VALUE bapieikp( ).
    TYPES return2                TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiret2 WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    " types return  type standard table of bapiret2 with default key.
    TYPES poitem                 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoitem WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poitemx                TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoitemx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poaddrdelivery         TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoaddrdelivery WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poschedule             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimeposchedule WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poschedulex            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimeposchedulx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poaccount              TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoaccount WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poaccountprofitsegment TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoaccountprofitsegment WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poaccountx             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepoaccountx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES pocondheader           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepocondheader WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES pocondheaderx          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepocondheaderx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES pocond                 TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepocond WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES pocondx                TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepocondx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES polimits               TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiesuhc WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES pocontractlimits       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiesucc WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poservices             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiesllc WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES posrvaccessvalues      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiesklc WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poservicestext         TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapieslltx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES extensionin            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiparex WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    " types extensionout    type standard table of bapiparex with default key.
    TYPES poexpimpitem           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapieipo WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poexpimpitemx          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapieipox WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES potextheader           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepotextheader WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES potextitem             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepotext WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES allversions            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimedcm_allversions WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES popartner              TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekkop WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES pocomponents           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepocomponent WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES pocomponentsx          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimepocomponentx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poshipping             TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiitemship WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poshippingx            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiitemshipx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES poshippingexp          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimeposhippexp WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES serialnumber           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimeposerialno WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES serialnumberx          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapimeposerialnox WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES invplanheader          TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapi_invoice_plan_header WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES invplanheaderx         TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapi_invoice_plan_headerx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES invplanitem            TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapi_invoice_plan_item WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES invplanitemx           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapi_invoice_plan_itemx WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    TYPES nfmetallitms           TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF /nfm/bapidocitm WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    DATA return2                TYPE return2.
*    DATA RETURN                 TYPE RETURN.
    DATA poitem                 TYPE poitem.
    DATA poitemx                TYPE poitemx.
    DATA poaddrdelivery         TYPE poaddrdelivery.
    DATA poschedule             TYPE poschedule.
    DATA poschedulex            TYPE poschedulex.
    DATA poaccount              TYPE poaccount.
    DATA poaccountprofitsegment TYPE poaccountprofitsegment.
    DATA poaccountx             TYPE poaccountx.
    DATA pocondheader           TYPE pocondheader.
    DATA pocondheaderx          TYPE pocondheaderx.
    DATA pocond                 TYPE pocond.
    DATA pocondx                TYPE pocondx.
    DATA polimits               TYPE polimits.
    DATA pocontractlimits       TYPE pocontractlimits.
    DATA poservices             TYPE poservices.
    DATA posrvaccessvalues      TYPE posrvaccessvalues.
    DATA poservicestext         TYPE poservicestext.
    DATA extensionin            TYPE extensionin.
*DATA extensionout           TYPE extensionout          .
    DATA poexpimpitem           TYPE poexpimpitem.
    DATA poexpimpitemx          TYPE poexpimpitemx.
    DATA potextheader           TYPE potextheader.
    DATA potextitem             TYPE potextitem.
    DATA allversions            TYPE allversions.
    DATA popartner              TYPE popartner.
    DATA pocomponents           TYPE pocomponents.
    DATA pocomponentsx          TYPE pocomponentsx.
    DATA poshipping             TYPE poshipping.
    DATA poshippingx            TYPE poshippingx.
    DATA poshippingexp          TYPE poshippingexp.
    DATA serialnumber           TYPE serialnumber.
    DATA serialnumberx          TYPE serialnumberx.
    DATA invplanheader          TYPE invplanheader.
    DATA invplanheaderx         TYPE invplanheaderx.
    DATA invplanitem            TYPE invplanitem.
    DATA invplanitemx           TYPE invplanitemx.
    DATA nfmetallitms           TYPE nfmetallitms.

    poheader = VALUE #( BASE CORRESPONDING #( po_header )
                        created_by = cl_abap_syst=>get_user_name( ) ).
    set_default_bapiupdate_flags( EXPORTING is_bapi_parameter   = poheader
                                  CHANGING  cs_bapi_parameter_x = poheaderx ).
    poitem = CORRESPONDING #( po_items ).
    LOOP AT poitem REFERENCE INTO DATA(line_poitem).
      DATA(line_poitemx) = VALUE bapimepoitemx( po_item = line_poitem->po_item ).
      set_default_bapiupdate_flags( EXPORTING is_bapi_parameter   = line_poitem->*
                                    CHANGING  cs_bapi_parameter_x = line_poitemx ).
      INSERT line_poitemx INTO TABLE poitemx.
    ENDLOOP.

    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CREATE1'
      EXPORTING
        poheader               = poheader
        poheaderx              = poheaderx
        poaddrvendor           = poaddrvendor
        testrun                = testrun
        memory_uncomplete      = memory_uncomplete
        memory_complete        = memory_complete
        poexpimpheader         = poexpimpheader
        poexpimpheaderx        = poexpimpheaderx
        versions               = versions
        no_messaging           = no_messaging
        no_message_req         = no_message_req
        no_authority           = no_authority
        no_price_from_po       = no_price_from_po
        park_complete          = park_complete
        park_uncomplete        = park_uncomplete
      IMPORTING
        exppurchaseorder       = exppurchaseorder
        expheader              = expheader
        exppoexpimpheader      = exppoexpimpheader
      TABLES
        return                 = return2
        poitem                 = poitem
        poitemx                = poitemx
        poaddrdelivery         = poaddrdelivery
        poschedule             = poschedule
        poschedulex            = poschedulex
        poaccount              = poaccount
        poaccountprofitsegment = poaccountprofitsegment
        poaccountx             = poaccountx
        pocondheader           = pocondheader
        pocondheaderx          = pocondheaderx
        pocond                 = pocond
        pocondx                = pocondx
        polimits               = polimits
        pocontractlimits       = pocontractlimits
        poservices             = poservices
        posrvaccessvalues      = posrvaccessvalues
        poservicestext         = poservicestext
        extensionin            = extensionin
        extensionout           = extensionout
        poexpimpitem           = poexpimpitem
        poexpimpitemx          = poexpimpitemx
        potextheader           = potextheader
        potextitem             = potextitem
        allversions            = allversions
        popartner              = popartner
        pocomponents           = pocomponents
        pocomponentsx          = pocomponentsx
        poshipping             = poshipping
        poshippingx            = poshippingx
        poshippingexp          = poshippingexp
        serialnumber           = serialnumber
        serialnumberx          = serialnumberx
        invplanheader          = invplanheader
        invplanheaderx         = invplanheaderx
        invplanitem            = invplanitem
        invplanitemx           = invplanitemx
        nfmetallitms           = nfmetallitms.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
      EXPORTING
        wait = 'X'.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD change.
    TYPES bapiekpo_t TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiekpo WITH DEFAULT KEY.
    DATA lt_msg_bapi TYPE bapiret2_t.

    DATA(purchaseorder) = EXACT ebeln( '9500000140' ).
    DATA(po_items) = VALUE bapiekpo_t( ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_GETDETAIL2'
      EXPORTING purchaseorder              = purchaseorder
                items                      = 'X'
      TABLES    po_items                   = po_items.
    data(new_po_item) = exact ekpo-ebelp( po_items[ lines( po_items ) ]-po_item ).
    new_po_item += 10.
    DATA(ls_item) = po_items[ 1 ].
    DATA(lt_poitem) = VALUE bapimepoitem_tp( ).
    DATA(lt_poitemx) = VALUE bapimepoitemx_tp( ).
    DATA(poitemx) = VALUE bapimepoitemx( ).
    DATA(poitem) = VALUE bapimepoitem( BASE CORRESPONDING #( ls_item )
                                       po_item = new_po_item ).
    INSERT poitem INTO TABLE lt_poitem.
    set_default_bapiupdate_flags( EXPORTING is_bapi_parameter   = poitem
                                  CHANGING  cs_bapi_parameter_x = poitemx ).
    poitemx-po_item = new_po_item.
*    poitemx-po_itemx = 'X'. " USELESS!? (https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-forum/bapi-po-change-meaning-of-x-key-fields-of-the-x-table-parameters/m-p/14360432)
    INSERT poitemx INTO TABLE lt_poitemx.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PO_CHANGE'
      EXPORTING purchaseorder = purchaseorder
      TABLES    return        = lt_msg_bapi
                poitem        = lt_poitem
                poitemx       = lt_poitemx.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
      EXPORTING wait = 'X'.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD set_default_bapiupdate_flags.
    DATA(lo_rtts) = CAST cl_abap_structdescr( cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data( cs_bapi_parameter_x ) ).
    LOOP AT lo_rtts->get_components( ) REFERENCE INTO DATA(ls_component).
      IF ls_component->type->get_relative_name( ) <> 'BAPIUPDATE'.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.
      ASSIGN is_bapi_parameter-(ls_component->name) TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lv_bapi_parameter_field>).
      IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR <lv_bapi_parameter_field> IS INITIAL.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.
      ASSIGN cs_bapi_parameter_x-(ls_component->name) TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lv_bapi_parameter_x_field>).
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CONTINUE.
      ENDIF.
      <lv_bapi_parameter_x_field> = abap_true.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Our 2026 Roadmap for Joule for Developers ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content:
Sapphire 2026 is approaching quickly and as usual it will bring lots of new announcements. So now is the right point in time to recap what has been announced already until Q2/2026.

The ABAP Platform AI (covering both SAP BTP ABAP Environment and SAP S/4HANA) transitions from a set of independent AI skills to full-scale Agentic AI. The roadmap focuses heavily on developer productivity via Joule and the modernization of ABAP development tools. The key benefits for customers and partners of the roadmap until Q2/2026 are:

 

  1. ABAP development tools in VS Code with ABAP MCP Server.
  2. ABAP AI for lower releases of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (i.e. 2021, 2022, 2023 and 2025).
  3. Simplified customer on-boarding of ABAP AI via SAP for Me and Central Business Configuration.
  4. Faster innovation with Side-by-Side consumption of SAP and 3rd party Agentic IDEs and LLMs
    (e.g. Microsoft, Amazon, IBM, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral…)
 

The biggest shift in 2026 will be the move toward Agentic AI, where the existing ABAP AI skills doesn't just explain, complete or generate code (e.g. RAP, CDS, Tests) independently but acts as an autonomous collaborator. This includes built-in ABAP MCP (Model Context Protocol) server. The introduction of VS Code as IDE can only be a start as the depth and breath of Eclipse which we achieved in so many years cannot be reached with one or two steps in VS Code. It'll be a journey which starts with first providing End-to-end UI services development with agents.

The new ABAP AI side-by-side architecture is designed to provide a scalable and future‑proof foundation for ABAP AI capabilities across heterogeneous SAP landscapes. It enables customers to consume ABAP AI services side‑by‑side, independent of the underlying S/4HANA release, ensuring access even for lower‑release Private Edition systems. 

A core benefit of the architecture is also its simplified enablement model through SAP for Me and Central Business Configuration, reducing on-boarding effort and ensuring consistent lifecycle management across environments. 
In addition, the architecture allows us to accelerate innovation by allowing customers to leverage SAP‑provided ABAP AI models alongside leading third‑party LLMs (e.g., Microsoft, Amazon, IBM, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral) more independently from releases. This flexibility ensures rapid adoption of new capabilities without dependency on individual release cycles.

 
Here you can learn more:
  1. SAP Joule for Developers (J4D) Expands to Private Cloud: Accelerating ABAP Innovation and Transformation
  2. Your Roadmap for getting started with ABAP AI
  3. ABAP AI Revolution Accelerates: The ABAP Developer Who Built Enterprise Apps in Minutes
  4. Joule for Developers and ABAP AI capabilities are coming to SAP S/4HANA Private Edition 2021, 2022, and 2023
  5. Public Roadmap: SAP Help / SAP Roadmap Explorer
  6. Help & Guides
    1. Step-by-step guide how to activate SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
    2. Joule for Developers (J4D) for S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup Guide
    3. SAP ABAP AI Discovery Center
    4. SAP BTP Discovery Center (Free Tier Services)
    5. Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
    6. YouTube playlist for Joule for Developers (J4D)
    7. Joule for Developers Help (J4D)
    8. Build SAP Fiori Apps with ABAP Cloud and SAP Joule for developers (RAP120) 
    9. ADT Eclipse AI Capabilities Help
    10. ABAP AI SDK Help
    11. ABAP-1 Help

More high-level insights about Joule for Developers/ABAP AI and ABAP platform can be found here . Here you find more on ABAP- 1,

entry.title: Re: Updating Production Order Component Quantity as per B...
entry.content:
Dear @Yash_Rudraksha,

SAP does not provide a released standard interface to add new standard component rows to the Components tab of an existing production order from a custom RAP application. In the released Production Order API (API_PRODUCTION_ORDER_2_SRV), SAP explicitly documents that production order components cannot be created they can only be updated or deleted via entity A_ProductionOrderComponent_4. SAP also provides released extensibility options around manufacturing orders, including header initialization, check-before-save, goods movement influence during confirmation and component-level custom field handling but SAP does not document any released Badi for appending new standard component rows to an existing production order. Therefore for this requirement, standard SAP does not offer a released API, Badi or other released transactional interface that would allow each custom batch-wise line item to be pushed as a new row into the standard Production Order Components tab. Standard users may add components manually in the UI but this capability is not exposed as a released programmatic interface for custom RAP applications

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/0a4262096d2e43258df27c594bdd1468.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/ed408e9ecb144e6eb09e6883d25a7262.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c20b8cfa8000468d86a005ec6d03259f.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: What Is Agentic AI and Why SAP Teams Are Paying Attention in 2026
entry.content:
Excellent analysis, Venkatesan. The evolution toward Agentic AI in 2026 is indeed a major milestone for the SAP ecosystem.

Drawing from +25 years of experience within SAP, I’ve observed that the success of these autonomous systems won’t just depend on their technical capabilities, but on the Agentic Governance frameworks surrounding them.

However, for most organizations, building this governance 'from scratch' is not a viable option. In my view, the real accelerator for adoption will be the availability of SAP Best Practices for Agentic AI. Customers will need a standardized baseline—leveraging tools like SAP BTP and SAP Cloud ALM—to define clear, pre-validated 'guardrails.'

This approach ensures that intelligent agents operate within strict financial and regulatory compliance boundaries without requiring each enterprise to reinvent the wheel. The focus should be on how we adapt these Best Practices to specific business contexts, ensuring that 'autonomous' never means 'uncontrolled.'

Looking forward to seeing how SAP further standardizes these governance templates to help teams deploy at scale with confidence.

entry.title: SAP RPT-1 Meets Employee Attrition: A Hands-On Test with SuccessFactors Data
entry.content:
The Short Version

I wanted to see if SAP’s new tabular AI model, RPT-1, could predict which employees are likely to quit, using nothing but a CSV extracted from SuccessFactors fields. No model training. No data science team. Just upload and predict.

To keep things honest, I based the experiment on a real peer-reviewed study that used actual employee data and traditional ML. The result? RPT-1 nailed all 5 test predictions in under 400 milliseconds. But the full story is more interesting than the headline.

 

Why I Picked Attrition (and Not Another Sales Demo)

Credit where it's due: Hari Panakkal's recent SAP Community post, Experiment with SAP RPT-1: Predicting the Right Carrier in SAP TM Using the No-Code Playground, is a great walkthrough of RPT-1 on transportation data. It got me thinking: if RPT-1 works that well for carrier prediction in TM, what happens when you point it at an HR problem?

Most RPT-1 demos I've seen focus on sales forecasting or logistics. Those are solid use cases, but the one I care about is people analytics, and the single biggest question HR teams ask is: "Who's about to leave?"

Attrition is a natural fit for RPT-1 because:

  • The data is tabular and already sitting in SuccessFactors
  • The problem is binary classification (stays vs. leaves)
  • Most HR teams don't have ML engineers on staff

But I didn't want to just throw random HR data at it. I wanted to test RPT-1 against something with a known answer.

 

The Research Behind the Experiment

I grounded everything in this study:

Manafi Varkiani, S., Pattarin, F., Fabbri, T., & Fantoni, G. (2025). Predicting employee attrition and explaining its determinants. Expert Systems With Applications, 272, 126575.

Here’s what makes this paper worth paying attention to:

It used real data. Not overused Kaggle dataset, but actual records from an Italian financial company. 5,767 employees. 309 voluntary resignations (a 5.4% attrition rate, which is realistic and makes the problem genuinely hard).

They tested four algorithms head-to-head. Random Forest crushed everything: 99.9% AUC, 94.4% sensitivity. Logistic Regression and Decision Trees weren’t even close.

They found things that don’t match your intuition. This is the best part. Using SHAP analysis, they discovered that employees who received bonuses were more likely to leave (burnout from overperforming), and employees who got retention incentives also showed higher attrition. It turns out retention incentives are targeted at people already flagged as flight risks, so the incentive is a signal of existing risk, not a cause of it.

The ML pipeline was serious work. ROSE resampling for class imbalance, 10-fold cross-validation, hyperparameter tuning, and SHAP post-processing. This took significant data science effort.

So the question became: Can RPT-1 get comparable results with a single API call?

 

Mapping the Variables to SuccessFactors

The study used 14 employee attributes. I mapped each one to the actual OData entity and field in SuccessFactors, and validated every mapping against the live API:

 

Research Variable

Where It Lives in SF

Category

Gender

PerPersonal.gender

Personal

Age Class

Derived from PerPerson.dateOfBirth

Personal

Tenure Class

Derived from EmpEmployment.startDate

People

Department

EmpJob.department

Work Environment

Rank

EmpJob.payGrade → FOPayGrade (grouped)

Management

Contract Type

EmpJob.contractType

Management

Job Level

EmpJob.payGrade

Management

Talent Program

TalentPool membership

People

Bonus Received

EmpPayCompNonRecurring existence

Management

Hours (PT/FT)

EmpJob.standardHours

Management

Retention Incentive

Custom MDF or EmpPayCompRecurring

Management

Job Family

FOJobCode.jobFunction

Management

Log Salary

EmpPayCompRecurring.paycompvalue (log10)

Management

Log Premium

Performance bonus (log10)

Management

Picture 1: Ishikawa – Fishbone diagram

 

What Is RPT-1, Exactly?

SAP RPT-1 is a Relational Pretrained Transformer , a foundation model built for tabular data, not text. Announced at TechEd 2025, it comes in three flavors:

  • sap-rpt-1-small : Fast commercial model (low latency)
  • sap-rpt-1-large : Higher accuracy commercial model
  • sap-rpt-1-oss : Open-source on GitHub (Apache 2.0)

The key idea: you give it labeled rows as examples and rows with [PREDICT] placeholders as questions. It learns from the context: no training, no feature engineering, no resampling. Think of it like few-shot prompting in ChatGPT, but for spreadsheets.

 

Running the Experiment

Step 1: Build the Dataset

I created sf_employee_attrition.csv with 50 labeled employee records using the 14 features from the research, plus 5 query rows with [PREDICT] in the ATTRITION column. The labeled data reflects the patterns the study found: higher attrition among younger, shorter-tenure employees with lower compensation, and the counterintuitive bonus/retention effects.

 

Picture 2 - sf_employee_attrition.csv, 50 labeled + 5 query rows, 14 features mapped to SF fields

 

Step 2: The No-Code Route (Playground)

Easiest way to test. Go to rpt.cloud.sap/dashboard:

  1. Upload the CSV
  2. The interface detects the [PREDICT] cells
  3. Click Predict
  4. Done

No coding. Supports up to 2,073 rows and 50 columns.

 

Step 3: The API Route (for Pipelines)

This option allows full control and evaluation metrics:

"""
SAP RPT-1 Employee Attrition Prediction
========================================
This script demonstrates how to use SAP-RPT-1 (Relational Pretrained Transformer)
to predict employee attrition using structured HR data modeled after
SuccessFactors workforce analytics dimensions.

Dataset design informed by: Manafi Varkiani et al. (2025),
"Predicting employee attrition and explaining its determinants",
Expert Systems With Applications, 272, 126575.


Prerequisites:
  - For API approach: SAP RPT Playground account (https://rpt.cloud.sap)
  - For OSS approach: Python 3.11+, GPU with 24-80GB VRAM recommended

Author: Shakti Barath
"""

import json
import csv
import requests
import os
from pathlib import Path

# ============================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================

# Option A: Load token from file (recommended)
# Create access_token.json with: {"token": "YOUR_TOKEN_HERE"}
# Get your token from: https://rpt.cloud.sap/docs (scroll to bottom)
TOKEN_FILE = "access_token.json"

# Option B: Set as environment variable
# export SAP_RPT_TOKEN="your_token_here"

RPT_API_URL = "https://rpt.cloud.sap/api/predict"
CSV_FILE = "sf_employee_attrition.csv"
TARGET_COLUMN = "ATTRITION"
PREDICT_PLACEHOLDER = "[PREDICT]"

def load_token():
    """Load API token from file or environment variable."""
    if os.path.exists(TOKEN_FILE):
        with open(TOKEN_FILE) as f:
            data = json.load(f)
            return data.get("token", "")
    return os.environ.get("SAP_RPT_TOKEN", "")

def load_csv_as_rows(filepath):
    """
    Load CSV and convert to RPT-1 row format.
    
    RPT-1 expects rows as a list of dicts. Rows with [PREDICT] in the 
    target column serve as query rows; all others are context rows
    (few-shot examples for in-context learning).
    """
    rows = []
    with open(filepath, newline="", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        reader = csv.DictReader(f)
        for row in reader:
            rows.append(dict(row))
    return rows

def build_payload(rows, target_column, index_column="EMP_ID"):
    """
    Build the RPT-1 API request payload.
    
    Structure:
    - rows: list of dicts (context + query rows)
    - index_column: unique identifier column
    - prediction_config: specifies target and task type
    
    Query rows contain [PREDICT] in the target column.
    Context rows contain actual labels — these are the few-shot examples.
    """
    payload = {
        "rows": rows,
        "index_column": index_column,
        "prediction_config": {
            "target_columns": [
                {
                    "name": target_column,
                    "placeholder_value": PREDICT_PLACEHOLDER,
                    "task_type": "classification"
                }
            ]
        }
    }
    return payload

def call_rpt1_api(payload, token):
    """Send prediction request to SAP RPT-1 Playground API."""
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "Accept": "application/json"
    }
    
    print("Sending prediction request to SAP RPT-1...")
    print(f"  Context rows: {sum(1 for r in payload['rows'] if r.get(TARGET_COLUMN) != PREDICT_PLACEHOLDER)}")
    print(f"  Query rows:   {sum(1 for r in payload['rows'] if r.get(TARGET_COLUMN) == PREDICT_PLACEHOLDER)}")
    
    response = requests.post(RPT_API_URL, headers=headers, json=payload, timeout=120)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.json()

def display_predictions(result, rows):
    """Display RPT-1 predictions alongside employee context."""
    predictions = result.get("prediction", {}).get("predictions", [])
    
    # Build lookup for employee context
    emp_lookup = {str(r["EMP_ID"]): r for r in rows}
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
    print("SAP RPT-1 ATTRITION PREDICTIONS")
    print("=" * 80)
    
    for pred in predictions:
        emp_id = str(pred.get("id", ""))
        emp = emp_lookup.get(emp_id, {})
        attrition_pred = pred.get(TARGET_COLUMN, [{}])
        
        if isinstance(attrition_pred, list) and len(attrition_pred) > 0:
            predicted_value = attrition_pred[0].get("prediction", "N/A")
            confidence = attrition_pred[0].get("confidence")
        else:
            predicted_value = "N/A"
            confidence = None
        
        risk_indicator = "HIGH RISK" if predicted_value == "Yes" else "LOW RISK"
        
        print(f"\n  Employee {emp_id}: {emp.get('JOB_FAMILY', 'Unknown')}")
        print(f"  Department: {emp.get('DEPARTMENT', '-')} | "
              f"Age: {emp.get('AGE_CLASS', '-')} | "
              f"Tenure: {emp.get('TENURE_CLASS', '-')}")
        print(f"  Talent Program: {emp.get('TALENT_PROGRAM', '-')} | "
              f"Contract: {emp.get('CONTRACT_TYPE', '-')}")
        print(f"  Prediction: {predicted_value} ({risk_indicator})")
        if confidence is not None:
            print(f"  Confidence: {confidence:.2%}")
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 80)

def analyze_context_patterns(rows):
    """
    Analyze attrition patterns in context data.
    Mirrors the descriptive statistics approach from the research paper
    (Manafi Varkiani et al., 2025, Table 6).
    """
    context_rows = [r for r in rows if r.get(TARGET_COLUMN) != PREDICT_PLACEHOLDER]
    
    print("\n" + "-" * 60)
    print("CONTEXT DATA ANALYSIS (mirrors research methodology)")
    print("-" * 60)
    
    # Attrition by age class
    age_stats = {}
    for r in context_rows:
        age = r["AGE_CLASS"]
        left = r[TARGET_COLUMN] == "Yes"
        if age not in age_stats:
            age_stats[age] = {"total": 0, "left": 0}
        age_stats[age]["total"] += 1
        age_stats[age]["left"] += int(left)
    
    print("\nAttrition Rate by Age Class:")
    for age in sorted(age_stats.keys()):
        s = age_stats[age]
        rate = (s["left"] / s["total"] * 100) if s["total"] > 0 else 0
        print(f"  {age:12s}: {rate:5.1f}%  ({s['left']}/{s['total']})")
    
    # Attrition by tenure class
    tenure_stats = {}
    for r in context_rows:
        tenure = r["TENURE_CLASS"]
        left = r[TARGET_COLUMN] == "Yes"
        if tenure not in tenure_stats:
            tenure_stats[tenure] = {"total": 0, "left": 0}
        tenure_stats[tenure]["total"] += 1
        tenure_stats[tenure]["left"] += int(left)
    
    print("\nAttrition Rate by Tenure Class:")
    for tenure in sorted(tenure_stats.keys()):
        s = tenure_stats[tenure]
        rate = (s["left"] / s["total"] * 100) if s["total"] > 0 else 0
        print(f"  {tenure:12s}: {rate:5.1f}%  ({s['left']}/{s['total']})")
    
    # Key insight from the research
    print("\n  Key Insight (Manafi Varkiani et al., 2025):")
    print("  Younger employees and those with shorter tenure show")
    print("  significantly higher attrition rates. The research found")
    print("  15.9% attrition for age 18-25 vs 1.4% for age 50-65.")

# ============================================================
# MAIN EXECUTION
# ============================================================

def main():
    print("=" * 80)
    print("SAP RPT-1: Employee Attrition Prediction Experiment")
    print("Grounded in: Manafi Varkiani et al. (2025), ESWA 272")
    print("=" * 80)
    
    # Load data
    if not Path(CSV_FILE).exists():
        print(f"ERROR: {CSV_FILE} not found. Place it in the same directory.")
        return
    
    rows = load_csv_as_rows(CSV_FILE)
    print(f"\nLoaded {len(rows)} employee records from {CSV_FILE}")
    
    # Analyze context patterns
    analyze_context_patterns(rows)
    
    # Load token
    token = load_token()
    if not token:
        print("\n" + "-" * 60)
        print("NO API TOKEN FOUND")
        print("-" * 60)
        print("To run predictions with the RPT Playground API:")
        print("1. Sign up at https://rpt.cloud.sap")
        print("2. Copy your token from https://rpt.cloud.sap/docs")
        print("3. Save it to access_token.json:")
        print('   {"token": "YOUR_TOKEN_HERE"}')
        print("\nAlternatively, use the OSS version (see rpt1_oss_predict.py)")
        return
    
    # Build payload and predict
    payload = build_payload(rows, TARGET_COLUMN)
    
    try:
        result = call_rpt1_api(payload, token)
        display_predictions(result, rows)
        
        # Save raw response
        with open("rpt1_predictions.json", "w") as f:
            json.dump(result, f, indent=2)
        print(f"\nRaw predictions saved to rpt1_predictions.json")
        
    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
        print(f"\nAPI Error: {e}")
        print("Check your token and ensure the playground is accessible.")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"\nError: {e}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Create access_token.json in the same folder with your RPT-1 API key:

{
  "token": "YOUR_RPT-API_KEY"
}

 

The Results

I ran the CSV through the RPT Playground API. Here’s exactly what came back:

EMP ID

Profile

Prediction

Confidence

Why It Makes Sense

1051

Finance • Staff • Permanent

Yes

95%

Age 25–30, Tenure 0–3 yr, Talent Program, Low premium

1052

IT • Staff • Permanent

No

98%

Age 30–40, Tenure 10–20 yr, Bonus received, Strong salary

1053

Sales • Staff • Fixed-Term

Yes

99%

Age 18–25, Tenure 0–3 yr, Fixed-term, Lowest salary

1054

HR • Manager • Permanent

No

99%

Age 40–50, Tenure 10–20 yr, Manager rank, High comp

1055

Ops • Staff • Permanent

Yes

94%

Age 25–30, Talent Prog, Retention incentive, Lower salary

 

Accuracy

Avg Confidence

Inference Time

Context Rows

5/5 (100%)

97%

399 ms

50 labeled rows

 

What Stood Out

The easy calls were easy. Employee 1053 (young, short-tenure, fixed-term, lowest salary) and Employee 1054 (senior manager, long tenure, high pay) both got 99% confidence. These are the extremes, exactly where the research paper’s Random Forest also showed the clearest separation.

Employee 1055 was the interesting one. This person has moderate tenure (3–10 years), which in the training data strongly correlates with staying. But RPT-1 correctly flagged them as at-risk at 94% confidence. Why? The combination of a retention incentive, talent program membership, and lower compensation. Remember what the research found: retention incentives are a signal that someone is already a flight risk, not a shield against it. RPT-1 picked up that interaction from just 50 context rows. That’s impressive.

399 milliseconds. The research paper required ROSE resampling, 10-fold cross-validation, hyperparameter tuning across four algorithms, and SHAP post-processing. RPT-1 got directionally equivalent predictions with a single API call on raw CSV data. For an HR team that wants a quick risk screening before committing to a full ML build, that’s a big deal.

The honest caveat: This is a 50-row synthetic dataset with intentionally clear patterns. Production data with 5,767 employees and a 5.4% attrition rate would be a harder test. These results show RPT-1 can read tabular patterns well. A head-to-head on real production data is the next step.

 

What RPT-1 Is Good At Here

  • No ML pipeline required. The research team spent serious effort on resampling, cross-validation, and algorithm selection. RPT-1 skips all of that.
  • Handles mixed data out of the box. Categorical and numerical fields go in as-is. No encoding needed.
  • Tolerates missing data. The research had to drop 5 variables due to gaps. RPT-1 handles missing values by design.
  • Easy to iterate. Want to add engagement survey scores? Just add a column. No pipeline rebuild.

 

What RPT-1 Can’t Do (Yet)

  • No explainability. RPT-1 gives you a prediction and a confidence score, but not why. For HR decisions, that matters a lot.
  • Class imbalance is your problem. With a 5.4% attrition rate, your context rows need careful curation.
  • No time-series awareness. RPT-1 works on static snapshots. It can’t predict when someone will leave.
  • Playground has limits. 2,073 rows max. For production scale, deploy via SAP AI Core.

 

So What Should You Actually Do With This?

RPT-1 makes it trivially easy to generate attrition predictions from SuccessFactors data. But predictions without understanding can lead to bad interventions. (Remember the retention incentive example: a naïve reading suggests incentives cause attrition.)

My recommendation:

  1. Use RPT-1 as a rapid screening tool. Upload your SF extract, get a risk score, identify who needs a closer look.
  2. Build a traditional ML model in parallel for explainability. Run the same features through Random Forest with SHAP to understand the why.
  3. Loop in HR. Especially for counterintuitive patterns. Data literacy plus domain knowledge turns a prediction into an action.
  4. Don’t treat aggregate results as universal. Attrition drivers for one job family can differ completely from the company-wide pattern.

 

References

  • Manafi Varkiani, S., Pattarin, F., Fabbri, T., & Fantoni, G. (2025). Predicting employee attrition and explaining its determinants. Expert Systems With Applications, 272, 126575.
  • Lundberg, S. M., & Lee, S. I. (2017). A Unified Approach to Interpreting Model Predictions. NeurIPS 2017.
  • SAP-RPT-1: sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/sap-rpt.html
  • SAP-RPT-1-OSS: github.com/SAP-samples/sap-rpt-1-oss
  • RPT Playground: rpt.cloud.sap
  • Reference Blog — Carrier Prediction in SAP TM: SAP Community (Mar 2026)

Happy experimenting!

Built with synthetic data mirroring the variable structure and patterns from Manafi Varkiani et al. (2025). No real employee data was used. Results are illustrative.

entry.title: Re: Personas script - Prevent users to reach Main Screen
entry.content:
Actually, the combination of suggestions from Cristin and Vladimirs achieve exactly what you want.

If you set the user's start transaction to /SCWM/RFUI and also use the ~singletransaction=1 and ~transaction=/SCWM/RFUI URL parameters, then it will behave like you intended. F7 on the initial screen will log off the user and when they log on again, they will go to /SCWM/RFUI, not to the SMEN screen. I tested this and it works.

If this is not how it works for you, then your Personas logoff is not configured properly. In case you are using Slipstream, you can refer to KBA 2911286 to verify what may be missing. If no SE, then there is probably a similar note/KBA explaining the same for ITS, but I don't have its number handy.

entry.title: How to fix Flexible Workflow for PO?
entry.content:
Solution

In order to have the Approver list show up in search Help you need to maintain both table SPOUSERAUTH and SPOPPWFL for PO Workflow and SPRUSERAUTH and SPRPPWFL  for PR Workflow. The value help works with a join of the above tables.

For other workflows

SRFQPPWFL People Picker for RFQ Approver
SRPLPRPPWFL Centrally Managed Pur Reqn User

SSCPPWFL People Picker for Supplier Confirmation A

entry.title: Re: Personas script - Prevent users to reach Main Screen
entry.content:
I think before trying to intercept F7, the combined use of start transaction and URL parameters should be tried first.

Should that not work for some reason, then you could even have an invisible custom control on the first screen and check its presence. To suppress the F7 action, you can use command "return true;". You need to do this tied to the onBeforeRequest screen event, and since that's executed at each screen change or user action, it does have some performance impact, but it shouldn't be too bad.

In general, follow Vladimirs' advice and try to avoid scripts assigned to screen events for best performance.

entry.title: Re: MM Status for "Intl Trade: Import" and "Intl Trade: E...
entry.content:
Hi @Devender_Budhiraja ,
My friend sap does not provide separate authorization statuses for the two International Trade views. In the authorization object M_MATE_STA, both views fall under the same Foreign Trade (FT) status group. That’s why you cannot find individual entries for Import vs. Export — sap treats them as one block. Both are controlled together under the Foreign Trade status. To control them separately, you must use a custom enhancement (user exit/BAdI) and a custom authorization object.

entry.title: Re: to set STO PO item level incoterm to blank while crea...
entry.content:
Hi @Abhishekramesh ,
My friend here’s the clearest way to handle this in SAP, making the Incoterm at STO PO item level blank during PO creation. Since SAP normally inherits Incoterms from the supplying plant’s vendor master or from configuration, you have to override that behavior. If your goal is simply to manually clear the Incoterm at item level, ensure this setting is enabled SPRO → Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Allow Incoterms at Item Level- Activate item-level Incoterms

Ensure field is optional, not required then in ME21N, go to Item → Delivery tab → Incoterms and delete the value If the field is required in your field selection, SAP will not allow blank.

Hope this will help you

entry.title: Re: Restricting Posing Date in MB51 Report
entry.content:
Hi @Mahil ,
My friend, here’s a clean and reliable way to enforce the “maximum one‑month posting date range” requirement for MB51 (Material Document List) in SAP. This is a classic enhancement scenario because MB51 itself does not provide configuration to restrict date ranges. MB51 uses the selection screen of program RM07DOCS.You can implement a check in one of the available enhancements:

Option 1 — User Exit EXIT_SAPMM07M_001 (Enhancement MBCF0002)
This exit is commonly used for validations related to material documents.

Option 2 — Implicit Enhancement in Program RM07DOCS
You can add a validation right after the selection screen input.

Accept my solution if it work for you.

entry.title: SAP Meetup Tbilisi
entry.content:
This year, we are bringing the SAP community back to Tbilisi — and this time in collaboration with New Vision University.

If you work with SAP, want to learn about it, or are simply curious about how global companies run their operations using enterprise technology, this meetup is for you.

Across the world, SAP communities regularly organize informal “Stammtisch”-style gatherings — spaces where people share knowledge, exchange experiences, discuss real projects, and support each other’s growth. Now, we are continuing to build that same spirit in Georgia and further strengthen the local SAP ecosystem together.

This meetup will bring together industry experts, universities, partners, and companies to explore how Georgia can position itself more strongly within the global SAP talent and innovation landscape.

Expect inspiring talks, practical insights, and the opportunity to connect with professionals already working with SAP technologies across different countries and industries.

Whether you are a student, developer, consultant, business professional, or simply curious — join us and become part of shaping the SAP Community Tbilisi.

Registration: Mandatory to attend - https://forms.gle/PeivJnzsT657AuhM7 

Location: New Vision University

When: April 24, 17:30

Beyond the meetup, we are also launching additional initiatives you can already register for:

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : How to determine if it's called from P...
entry.content:
This is not easy, because you would need to find out whether the current session is running via Personas (either the personas or slipstream service. It could be the webgui service as well, which will also use Personas flavors).

If you look into SM04, there is some indication in the "Application info." column that lets you find out which service an HTTPS type session is using. So based on this, I could imagine that the BAdI code could find out if it runs via something that MIGHT have a flavor active, but there are multiple difficulties with this. First, I don't know how to identify the current session in this list. Also, this doesn't indicate if a flavor is active or not. In other words, I think this would be rather tricky and I don't know how to determine what you're looking for. Perhaps someone else has a better idea 🙂

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : How to add functionality in table cell
entry.content:
Not sure which 'Overview" table do you mean in PA20, but it doesn't really matter. 

Whether you could use SAPUI5 applet easily or not, depends on your level of expertise with applets. I don't really have any, so cannot tell how easy this would be.

I believe it should be achievable, but this is just a guess on my part. Keep in mind that by using SAPUI5 applets in your flavor, you are restricting yourself to Slipstream only, since this Personas feature is not supported in webgui.

entry.title: Time zone conversion for message mapping in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Time zone conversion is not often used in Integration Suite, as date and time are mostly converted to different time zone in target system for most integrations. More recently however, with more standardized and cloud systems being used (e.g S4HANA Cloud), as well as prepackaged API's, time zone conversion has become more common. In addition, most countries use daylight savings time, which requires a different time offset depending on the day and month for date and time.

For SAP PO, the most common way to convert date and time to a different timezone is to use java libraries for Joda-Time in a user defined function to convert date and time to a different timezone. For Integration Suite, these libraries can be used by default in groovyscript. Following an earlier post with an example of how to use these libraries in groovy, as well as some changes to the code to make the function more generic, the code can be used in message mapping to convert date and time to a different timezone.

import java.util.TimeZone;
import org.joda.time.DateTime;
import org.joda.time.DateTimeZone;
import org.joda.time.format.DateTimeFormat;
import org.joda.time.format.DateTimeFormatter;

def String converttimezone(String input, String pattern, String totimezone) {
    
    //set output to empty
    def output = "";

    //if input is not empty, convert input to timezone
    if (input != "") {
        DateTimeFormatter dtformatter = DateTimeFormat.forPattern(pattern);
        DateTime datetime = dtformatter.parseDateTime(input);
        TimeZone timezone = TimeZone.getTimeZone(totimezone);
        output = datetime.withZone(DateTimeZone.forTimeZone(timezone)).toString();
    }
    
    return output;
}

The function uses parameters input, pattern and totimezone to convert a date and time to a different timezone

  • input: date and time with offset or timezone (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00)
  • pattern: format for date and time, using notation for SimpleDateFormat

    Most commonly used notation:

    • y - year
    • M - month
    • d - day
    • H - hour
    • m - minutes
    • s - seconds
    • S - milliseconds
    • Z - time offset (e.g +01:00, +0100)
    • ZZZ - time zone (e.g UTC, Europe/Amsterdam); this can be any identifier for time zone
      • Any text which is not part of the date and time notation can be enclosed in apostrophe (e.g yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss)
  • totimezone: any identifier for time zone (e.g UTC, Europe/Amsterdam)

The output date and time format for the function is always the same, either yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00Z) when converting date and time to UTC, or yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00) for any other time zone. If needed, the standard datetrans function can be used in message mapping to change the date and time to a different format.

Examples

Example 1: convert date and time with offset (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00) to UTC

In this case date and time with offset is used from source message and converted to UTC timezone

  • input: date and time with offset (2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00)
  • pattern: date and time with offset (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ)
  • totimezone: UTC

After converting date and time to UTC, the format is changed to remove milliseconds using datetrans function (format yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' to yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss)

Example 2: convert date and time without offset from timezone CET to timezone UTC

In this case date and time is used without offset and timezone has to be added before converting date and time to different timezone

  • input: date and time, timezone is CET and is added using concat function (e.g 2026-02-25 17:59:23 CET)
  • pattern: date and time with offset (yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss ZZZ)
  • totimezone: timezone is UTC

After converting date and time to UTC, format is changed back to original using datetrans function (format yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' to yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss)

Example 3: convert date and time from timezone UTC to timezone Europe/Amsterdam

  • input: date and time, timezone is added (e.g 2026-08-27T12:00:00 UTC)
  • pattern: date and time with timezone (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss ZZZ)
  • totimezone: date and time is set to timezone Europe/Amsterdam

Because offset can be different depending on the day and month for daylight savings time, milliseconds and offset are removed using substring function.

 

References:

  • Timezone conversion for SAP PO: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/time-zone-conversion-in-pi-mapping/ba-p/13156873
  • Earlier example of function to convert date and time to different timezone: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45834774/groovy-date-timezone-formatting
  • Full list of class SimpleDateFormat, used for date and time notation: http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html
  • List of all timezones: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

entry.title: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance Orders with SAP Field Service Management – Part 1
entry.content:
Introduction

In asset‑intensive industries such as utilities, renewable energy, transportation, and manufacturing, maintenance efficiency plays a decisive role in asset uptime and operational reliability. While SAP S/4HANA Asset Management focuses on planning, structuring, and settling maintenance processes, the actual execution often happens in the field — powered by SAP Field Service Management (FSM).

To avoid inconsistent data, delays, manual work, and visibility gaps, customers increasingly turn to end‑to‑end integration between S/4HANA and FSM. This blog explains the complete process, prerequisites, configuration steps, and execution flow for seamless replication of Maintenance Orders, Activities, Confirmations, Materials, Expenses, and Attachments between the systems.

Key Business Benefits of Integration

  1. Seamless Maintenance Execution: Integration enables a seamless flow from maintenance planning and work order creation in S/4HANA to service execution and confirmation in FSM, ensuring no loss of information across systems.
  2. Real-Time Asset Visibility: Field technicians receive accurate asset master data, functional locations, equipment history, and task instructions directly on their mobile devices, while updates from the field are synchronized back to S/4HANA in real time.
  3. Faster Response and Reduced Downtime: By combining asset intelligence with intelligent scheduling and dispatching, organizations can react quickly to asset issues, minimizing downtime and improving asset availability.
  4. Improved Technician Productivity: FSM provides optimized scheduling, route planning, and mobile execution capabilities, enabling technicians to complete jobs faster and more accurately.
  5. Accurate Cost and Compliance Tracking: Time, spare parts, and service confirmations captured in FSM are transferred back to S/4HANA Asset Management, ensuring accurate cost posting, maintenance history, and regulatory compliance.

Process Overview: S/4HANA to FSM: 

  • In SAP S/4HANA, when a maintenance order is saved, a corresponding service call and activities are automatically created in SAP Field Service Management based on the system statuses defined in the configuration. One or more system statuses can be configured to determine when a maintenance order should be replicated.
  • When a previously replicated maintenance order is saved again in SAP S/4HANA, the service call and associated activities in SAP Field Service Management are automatically updated based on the current system status of the maintenance order and the configured settings.​
  • All the stock & non-stock components from maintenance order operation are replicated to activity as reserved materials.​
  • When a maintenance order is marked as Technically Completed (TECO) in SAP S/4HANA, the status of the corresponding service call in SAP Field Service Management is automatically updated to Technically Completed, provided the statuses are mapped in the configuration. Additionally, all activities within the service call are set to CLOSED.​

Process Overview: FSM to S/4HANA

  • When a service call with type Maintenance is created in SAP Field Service Management, a corresponding maintenance order is created in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When a service call with type Maintenance is updated in SAP Field Service Management, the corresponding maintenance order is updated in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an activity is created in SAP Field Service Management, a corresponding order operation with status Created is created in the relevant maintenance order in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an activity of Service Call with type Maintenance is modified in SAP Field Service Management, the Description, Equipment, Duration, Planned Start and End Dates, and Person Responsible are transferred from the activity in SAP Field Service Management to the maintenance order operation in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an activity of Service Call with type Maintenance is being assigned to a technician and released in SAP Field Service Management, the corresponding Person Responsible is updated in the Maintenance Order Operation.​
  • After any time effort, materials (planned and unplanned), or expenses are approved in SAP Field Service Management, corresponding documents such as maintenance order confirmations, goods issue/ receipt documents, and G/L documents are created in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an attachment is uploaded to a service call or to an activity, the attachment is transferred to SAP S/4HANA.Service call or activity-level attachment replication is supported for maintenance orders and operations as well.​

Sequence of Operations

Prerequisites

  • The minimum SAP S/4HANA version must be 2023 FPS3.
  • Equipments and functional locations are already replicated to SAP FSM.
  • Material and business partners must already be present in SAP FSM
  • Ensure region code in SAP Field Service Management is manually created in SAP S/4HANA following the required naming convention. See more details.
  • CPI iFlows active and configured

Configurations

This section summarizes the technical setup required across S/4HANA, FSM, and CPI.

1 OAuth Config

Use T-code SOAUTH2_CLIENT to configure or view OAuth 2.0 Client Configuration

  • Click on create

  • Give below values for create
    • OAuth 2.0 Client Profile: FSM_PROFILE
    • Configuration: Custom name for your OAuth profile
    • OAuth 2.0 Client ID: Client ID from the FSM

  • Add token endpoint

  • Switch to ‘Client Authentication’ and select ‘Form Fields’. Edit to enter ‘Client Secret’

  • Save and exit the edit mode.

  • Click on ‘Token’ to test the token service. If Access Token comes up as valid then OAuth set up is working

2 Create RFC Connection

Use T-code SOAUTH2_CLIENT to configure or view OAuth 2.0 Client Configuration

  • Select ‘Create’

  • Give appropriate name and select Type G connection

  • Fill details like description and host name under Technical Settings

  • Under Logon & Security, select the OAuth profile created in the previous step

  • Under Logon & Security, change SSL to Active and choose PSE ID. In current system you can choose ANONYM SSL Client (Anonymous)

  • Save and perform connection test. You should get 200 OK response

3 Configure replication in SPRO

  • Configure Replication Parameters

Enter following values relevant for plant maintenance

Scope

Parameter

Value

GLOBAL

RFC

RFC destination created above

GLOBAL

X-Account-ID

Account ID of FSM

GLOBAL

X-Client-ID

Client ID generated in FSM

GLOBAL

X-Client-Version

0.0.1

GLOBAL

X-Company-ID

Company ID of FSM

MAIN_ORDER

S4_FSM_Company_ID

00001

MAIN_ORDER

X-Create-Or-Update

true

MAIN_ORDER

pathConfirmation

/api/fsm-connector/v1/confirmation/${ActivityId}/approvalitem

MAIN_ORDER

pathServiceRoot

/api/fsm-connector/v1/composite-tree/service-calls/

  • Define Plant Specific Order Types

Maintain plant and order type combination that needs to be replicated

S4 Cmp. ID

Type

Plant

00001

YBA1

1710

00001

PM01

1010

  • Define System Status on which maintenance order needs to be replicated.

S4 Cmp. ID

Type

Plant

00001

I0002

Replicate Object

  • Configure Mapping for Service Call Type Codelist

Maintain mapping between of maintenance order type and FSM service call type

  • Configure Mapping for Status Codelist

Maintain mapping between order status in S/4HANA and FSM

  • Configure Mapping for Priority Codelist

Maintain mapping between order priority in S/4HANA and FSM service call priority.

  • Configure Mapping for Problem Type Codelist

Maintain mapping between activity type of Order in S/4HANA and FSM problem type.

  • Configure Region

Ensure region code in SAP Field Service Management is manually created in SAP S/4HANA following the required naming convention based on following setting.

  • Set Default Business Partner (Customer)

Configure the default business partner that will be used in FSM for service call.

  • Configure Mapping for Expense Types

  • Configure for Expense document type item details

4 Set up Service call Type in FSM

      Set up service call type code in FSM settings and configuration by navigating as below in FSM

            Settings and Configuration -> Planning and Dispatching -> Service call -> Service Call Types

5 Set up Service Regions

Create service regions to be used for maintenance order replication. This set up depends on set up done for ‘Configure Region’ in S/4HANA. You can read more details at Create Regions for Maintenance Order Replication | SAP Help Portal

Typically the code will be in the form of WC_<Workcenter Plant>_<Workcenter at Order>
Please maintain at below path in FSM: Settings and Configuration -> Planning and Dispatching -> General Settings -> Service Regions

6 Configure iflows in CPI

Apart from relevant master data iflows, below iflows needs to be configured specifically for maintenance order integration. These iflows replicate updates and confirmation from FSM to S/4HANA.

  • Route Payload from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Route the Maintenance order updates to below maintenance order related iflows.
    • For this it needs only config addition as below to identify correct service call type related to maintenance order

  • Notify Plant Maintenance Order Update from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates the updates like activity assignment release, change in dates.
  • Replicate Plant Maintenance Call from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates newly created service call from FSM to S/4HANA.
  • Update Maintenance Order for New Activity from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates any newly created maintenance activity in FSM as operation to S/4HANA
  • Replicate Confirmed Maintenance from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates the confirmation approved by back office in FSM

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : How to determine if it's called from Personas flavor in BAdI
entry.content: Interesting question. From what I’ve seen, one common approach is to check the context or session details when the BAdI is triggered — sometimes you can identify if it’s coming from a Personas flavor by looking at specific parameters or UI-related indicators passed during execution. Another option could be checking for Personas-specific services or flags in the call stack, depending on how your system is configured. It’s not always super straightforward, but there’s usually some trace you can hook into. I’ve dealt with similar conditional logic in other platforms like phonexa.com, where you need to detect the source of execution to apply different behavior — same idea here, just within SAP’s environment.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
Integrating SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance Orders with SAP Field Service Management – Part 2
entry.content:
Demo

A renewable energy company operates multiple wind farms. Each wind turbine is managed as an equipment object in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management, with defined maintenance plans and performance thresholds.

A wind turbine’s temperature sensor detects that the temperature is higher than normal operating limits and required maintenance.

1 Creation of PM Notification (IW21)

·       Enter T-code IW21 and enter ‘Y1’ as Notification Type then hit Enter or F8

·       Enter Following details to create PM Notification

Short Text/Description: Turbine generator temperature over limit (Add different name to differentiate)

Functional Location, Equipment, Planner Group, Main WorkCtr, Required Endn Date, Final Due Date.

·       Click on ‘Issue Permit’ or Shift+F7

·       Click on ‘Create’ button next to Order

·       Enter YBA1 as value for Order Type and click continue

·       Under Operations tab, add below details to create first operation.

Operation Short Text: Turbine brush replacement

Work: 2 (or any number of hours)

Duration: 2 (or any number of hours)

·       Under Components tab, add below details to add a reserved material for operation.

Component: TURBINE_BRUSH

Requirement Quantity: 3

Storage: 171A

Plant: 1710

Activity: 10

·       You can release the maintenance order by clicking on Release or Ctrl+F1

·       Save the maintenance order and note the order number

2 Verify Service call in FSM

Navigate to service calls under Planning and Dispatching in FSM and filter with the help of External ID

  • Verify details like Equipment, Activities and reserved material under activity

3   Dispatch the activity to technician

    Navigate to Dispatching Board and filter the service call by using Order number as External ID

  • Drag and drop the activity to technician.

  • Release the assignment by clicking on Release Assignments on top right corner while selecting the dragged activity

  • Once released the activity will become visible on the windows app. Do note the activity number to continue with windows app

4 Work and add efforts on windows efforts

  • If app is already logged and initial sync is completed. Do a sync by clicking on synchronization button on top right

  • Once sync completed, you can search with activity code under Activities

  • Click on Work to change status of activity. Confirm any prompts (Do not use CHECKOUT option when available)
  • Click on Reserved Materials to note the use of material

  • Add Efforts by selecting Create Effort under Journal or by selecting Create Effort from Add menu at the bottom right

 

  • Add work time and select the task then save

     

  • Add Expense by selecting Create Expense under Journal or by selecting Create Effort from Add menu at the bottom right

  • Add expense like meals and save

  • Once the journal entries are added you can initiate the sync

5 Verify Time and Material as back office

  • Navigate to Time and Material Journal on the web app under Planning and Dispatching

  • Filter by either activity or service call code

  • Approve each item separately (This is a limitation on test system. In productive landscape bulk approval is possible). For each item provide approval notes

  • Once approved, confirmations will be visible in S4

6 Validate Goods Issue, confirmation and G/L account posting

  • Go to T-code IW33 and enter the order number to display the order
  • From the display of maintenance order, select Document flow or ctrl+F11

     

  • You can check Goods Issue and confirmation posted

  • You can verify more details by double clicking on GI and confirmation entries

  • To view the G/L posting, you can go to T-code FB02 and select Document list or shift+F8

  • Add below parameters for search

Company code: 1710

Fiscal Year: current year

Document Type: SA

Posting Date: Today

  • The exact document number can be verified in AIF Monitor

T-code: /O/AIF/ERR

Namespace: /FSMPM

Interface Name: CONFIRM_IN

Interface Version: 1

Generic Selection: Today

Status Selection: Select All

  • Verify the G/L posting for the amount posted from FSM as expense

7 Create new activity in FSM and replicate as operation

  • Create a new activity under same service call. Important information to add is as below
    • Subject: Example- Backup Battery replacement
      • Planned Duration: change hours to may be 5
      • Region: WC_1710_TATA_ELE
    • Verify the replication by checking the maintenance order (IW33) under operations. Do verify hours entered in FSM being replicated to S4
    • Release the activity to Technician
    • Add an un-planned material by choosing ‘Create Material’ and searching for Turbine Battery
    • Make sure to use material from warehouse 1710/171A

  • You can also add Effort for service hours and sync
  • Approve the material and effort in web app under Time and Material Journal. Do approve the entries one by one
  • Verify the replicated confirmation from document flow on display of maintenance order (IW33)     

8 Create new service call in FSM and replicate as maintenance order (exercise)

  • Create a new service call in FSM by clicking on ‘Create Service Call’ in service call screen under planning and dispatching
  • Give below details and save to create a service call

Subject: Any subject of choice

Type: Maintenance

Problem Type: Any (You can choose Mechanical)

Responsible: Technician Name

Customer: Default customer

  • Note the created service call code. Close and open the service call to check the updated external ID

  • In S/4, Navigate to display maintenance order (IW33) to verify the order replicated. You can create operations and add components to verify replication further

9 Add attachment from FSM

  • On the service call created in last step, add an attachment from Attachments tab
    • Adding attachment triggers replication, no extra step need to confirm the attachment addition in FSM
  • Verify if attached replicated to maintenance order by verifying under ‘Additional Data’ -> ‘Document Assignments’

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_schad,

Thank you for the blog and the clarifications regarding SAP Build Apps and its plans. While everyone understands and accepts that technology and tools evolve, the abrupt discontinuation of a tool that was heavily promoted as a "beautiful low-code, no-code solution" feels quite harsh. Clients would generally be more accepting if a new and improved tool were released. However, the announcement that there will be no future support for the existing tool—at least for the next three to five years, as is typical for other SAP products—combined with the urgent demand for customers to migrate all existing applications to a new tool, is concerning. I can only imagine how customers will react to such news!

Thanks and regards,  

Raja Sekhar K

entry.title: Re: BAPI_PO_CHANGE meaning of X key fields of the X table parameters
entry.content:
In BAPI_PO_CHANGE (e.g. POITEMX):

  • The system uses PO_ITEM to identify rows
  • Uses non-key X fields (e.g. DELETE_IND = 'X') to control updates
  • Does not check PO_ITEMX at all

Therefore, setting PO_ITEMX to 'X', space, or any value produces the same result.

They exist because SAP followed a generic rule:

Every field has a corresponding X field—even for key fields where it’s unnecessary.

Exception:
In more complex structures (e.g. conditions like POCONDX), SAP may build keys dynamically, and X key fields can influence:

  • Record matching
  • Update vs insert behavior

Finally,

  • For item-level tables → ignore X key fields
  • For dynamic structures (conditions/pricing) → they may matter

entry.title: Batch Determination Enhancement with Shelf-Life Freshness Control (≥75%)
entry.content:
Hello,

One Requirement related to Batch Determination but freshness control 75 come from customer 

Requirement -

 

The proposed solution in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud will introduce freshness-based batch control at the Sales Order level for Domestic customers. For MT, E-commerce, and CSD channels, batch determination will be mandatory and restricted to only those batches that meet a configurable minimum freshness threshold (default ≥75%), calculated based on the remaining shelf life derived from the batch expiration date and sales order date. This freshness percentage will be maintained at an administrative level for flexibility. The customer channel will be identified via a field in the Business Partner master, which will drive the applicability of this logic. For other channels such as GT, FS, and B2B, standard FIFO-based batch selection will continue without freshness restriction. This solution will apply only to domestic transactions, while export orders and branch transfers remain out of scope.

How can we configure this in SAP S/4 hana Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Creating Production Order Component Line Items via API
entry.content:
Dear @Yash_Rudraksha,

SAP does not provide a released standard POST/API to create new standard component rows in an existing production order via A_ProductionOrderComponent_4. The released API documentation for this entity covers read, update (PATCH) and delete (DELETE) but SAP does not expose a released create operation for inserting a new component line into an already existing production order

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/0a4262096d2e43258df27c594bdd1468.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/ed408e9ecb144e6eb09e6883d25a7262.html

If the additional lines must exist as part of the standard production order structure the supported Public Cloud approach is to create that structure upstream for example by using Create Planned Order with Components and then Convert to Production Order. SAP documents both released operations and SAP also states that the material components from the planned order are copied into the production order during conversion. If the real requirement is batch-wise execution or consumption handling rather than structurally inserting new standard component rows into the order the better Public Cloud design is to keep the standard order component structure unchanged and manage the batch split in the custom solution and/or confirmation logic. SAP released extensibility in this area is aimed at custom-field handling on order components and goods-movement behavior during confirmation not at creating new standard component items in an existing order through a released API

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/720170b7e5f14cf39c378606c53bb566.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/9622aec3c7ce4b83a9d00cfb46d72ff0.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/dd313229871540368053d17eacf1e2ea.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c20b8cfa8000468d86a005ec6d03259f.html

Summary:

SAP no released standard API is currently available to create new production order component lines in an existing order. The supported direction is either to build the component structure before production order creation or to keep the order structure standard and handle the batch-wise logic in the custom application design

Best Regards,

Dawid
