entry.title: How SAP’s New Scheduling Capabilities Are Transforming Cell & Gene Therapy Orchestration
entry.content:
Introduction

The advancement of cell and gene therapy has brought forth unique challenges in orchestrating personalized treatments, requiring seamless coordination between manufacturing, logistics, and clinical operations. SAP’s latest scheduling capabilities are designed to address these complexities, offering a robust solution that aims to streamline the entire therapy process for better patient outcomes.

SAP's cell and gene therapy orchestration solution, version 2511, addresses the complexity of personalized medicine with a scheduling engine that integrates manufacturing, logistics, and clinical timelines.

Precise scheduling is essential in cell and gene therapy, as each patient’s timeline must be tailored. For instance, if a biospecimen is collected on Monday, every subsequent step must be coordinated to ensure readiness for infusion the following week. Version 2511 will significantly benefit the cell and gene therapy community by establishing a foundation for automation and future enhancements.

A New Foundation: Scheduling in Release 2511

1. Intelligent Scheduling Engine

The new engine supports:

  • Soft vs. hard booking (tentative hold vs. confirmed reservation)
  • Rescheduling and cancellation (future expansions)
  • Partial scheduling
  • Calendar-driven capacity
  • Multiple manufacturing steps and multiple plant logic

CGTO now calculates multiple scheduling proposals, automatically factoring:

  • Available manufacturing slots
  • Lead times between milestones
  • Defined flow version steps
  • Material-specific capacity at each plant

This enables treatment centers to request time slot options for specific dates, such as biospecimen pickup, and receive immediate, feasible end-to-end timelines.

2. Soft to Hard Booking Workflow

Release 2511 introduces soft booking, which temporarily reserves a slot until the treatment order is created and approved. If approval is not received within the specified timeframe, the slot is automatically released.

This approach prevents bottlenecks and ensures equitable access for centers competing for limited manufacturing capacity.

3. Manufacturing Slot Management

The new Manage Manufacturing Slots application allows plant managers to:

  • Define and adjust daily slot capacities.
  • Associate slots with specific plants and materials
  • View both soft and hard bookings.
  • Automatically block consecutive days when manufacturing spans multiple days.

The system coordinates multi-step manufacturing across multiple plants, ensuring feasibility throughout the process.

4. Milestones & Lead Times

CGTO can now automatically generate standard therapy milestones such as:

  • Biospecimen pickup & delivery
  • Processing start & end
  • Finished product pickup & delivery

Administrators define:

  • Milestone sequence
  • Lead times between milestones
  • Milestones to include/exclude from scheduling

This logic enables the scheduling engine to simulate a complete patient treatment schedule, from specimen collection to therapy delivery, accounting for all required steps and time intervals.

Looking Forward: Roadmap 2605 and Beyond

SAP's Release 2605 roadmap includes enhancements such as custom milestones, partial scheduling, rescheduling and cancellation, a scheduler application, and external scheduling integration.

Customers will define milestones for adaptable scheduling across therapy models.

1. Partial Scheduling

Centers will be able to schedule specific parts of the therapy process, such as manufacturing or biospecimen collection.

2. Re-scheduling & Cancellation

Additional flexibility will be available through:

  • Automated rescheduling
  • Controlled cancellation flows

3. Scheduler’s Application

A new UI for schedulers will allow:

  • Visual slot management
  • Slot assignment
  • Real-time modifications
  • Exception handling

4. External Scheduling Engine Integration

The initial integration phase will allow CGTO to receive or exchange scheduling information with hospitals, laboratories, and manufacturing systems.

Conclusion: SAP Is Building a True End‑to‑End Scheduling Ecosystem

With Release 2511, SAP delivers essential CGT industry scheduling features. Planned Release 2605 enhancements—custom milestones, rescheduling, scheduler applications, and new integrations—further strengthen SAP CGTO as a leading orchestration engine for individualized therapies.

This advancement extends beyond scheduling by establishing a foundation for a digitally coordinated, patient-centric therapy process. SAP remains committed to delivering a high-quality solution for the field.

entry.title: Re: Setup and Activation Guide: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Thanks for this question.

Here are my finidings : 

When you have BTP Joule configured in one subaccount/region and need to use the Connectivity service from a different subaccount or region, you'll need to establish proper trust relationships and configure destinations to enable secure communication across these boundaries.

Steps for implementation

1. Setup Trust Between Subaccounts

2. Configure Destination in Source Subaccount

3. Cross-Region Authentication Setup

Please have a look at this recently released guide https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf

If the specific scenario is not covered then you can reach out to our product support as we may not have released a specific document for each scenario.

entry.title: Re: RAP Event: consume SAP standard event and trigger custom RAP event
entry.content:
Thank you very much for this @hironobu_nakayama, it works perfectly for my usecase (filter WBS element create /change events and add some data to the payload).

Only thing I'd point out is there is a missing IMPORTING keyword in the syntax given on this blog:

CLASS lcl_event_consump DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
      consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT changed_instances FOR purchaseorder~changed.
ENDCLASS.

Once I added the IMPORTING keyword, it works:

CLASS lcl_event_consump DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
      consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT IMPORTING changed_instances FOR purchaseorder~changed.
ENDCLASS.

 Nonetheless, great blog that opens so many possibilities for clean core development. Kudos!

entry.title: 4g group valuation ledger with 5w2
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are implementing sap s/4 hana public cloud for a customer who has operations in China and Malaysia. As for organization structure, it has 2 company codes for Chinese entities and 1 company code for Malaysia entity.

DEV-Customizing system is provisioned.  CNAP accounting principle is assigned to leading ledger 0L.  Scopse item 5W2 is activated in initial scoping when deploying DEV-Customizing system. 4G group valuation ledger is auto generated with the activation of 5W2.  Now there is error because of  inconsistency in configuration of ledger.  Pls suggest how to handle this situation what actions we should take now . Thank you! 

Error message: 

Configuration settings need to be corrected.
Message no. FINS_ACDOC_CUST201

Diagnosis
There are inconsistencies in the configuration settings for ledgers.

System Response
As long as configuration settings are inconsistent, postings are not allowed to prevent database mismatches.
